vitals.location: Peso da Régua, Douro Valley, Portugal
vitals.feed_zones: Electronic timing with feed stations along the course, per organizer regulations

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: All three routes start and finish in Peso da Régua. The Granfondo heads south through Lamego, Tarouca, Moimenta da Beira, São João da Pesqueira, Tabuaço and Armamar on paved, hilly Douro roads, following the EN226 toward Faia and the Vilar reservoir before returning through vineyard villages and Tabuaço to the Douro-side EN222. Terraced vineyards, river valleys, sustained climbing and steep descents define the route; the southern alignment was introduced after winter landslides damaged the traditional northern roads, and total elevation gain remains unpublished by the organizer. All three distances start together with only about 2 km controlled, creating a potentially crowded opening.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Tabuaço climb — 9 km at 5.6% average, the principal quantified climb on the course, followed by a steep, technical descent through Barcos, Adorigo and Tedo.
suffering_zones: EN226 from Régua through Lamego/Tarouca to Granja Nova — the long shared opening section
Moimenta da Beira-Faia-Vilar reservoir-Paredes da Beira-Espinhosa-Castanheiro do Sul — the extended Granfondo-only inland loop
Tabuaço-Barcos-Adorigo-Tedo descent — explicitly described by the organizer as steep, before the final EN222 run back toward Régua

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and guesthouses in Peso da Régua and nearby Douro Valley towns; book early due to wine tourism popularity.

history.origin_story: Part of Portugal's Superprestígio competitive series.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Real, Well-Attended Portuguese Fondo — With Data Gaps
biased_opinion.summary: Love Tiles Douro Granfondo is a legitimate, well-attended event through UNESCO-listed Alto Douro vineyard country, drawing roughly 2,000 riders from 25 nationalities and carrying real competitive credibility through Portugal's Superprestígio series — Rui Costa and Pepe have both been associated with the event. But the organizer's own published course figures don't agree with each other (140.3/105.4/71.1 km in official documentation versus 135/100/69 km elsewhere in the same narrative materials), and total elevation gain for the Granfondo route is not published anywhere found. The southern route alignment used in 2026 was itself a reroute forced by winter landslide damage to the traditional course.
strengths: UNESCO-listed Alto Douro vineyard scenery with terraced hills, river valleys and vineyard villages
Substantial international field — roughly 2,000 riders from 25 nationalities
Three route lengths with electronic timing, feed stations, medical and professional mechanical support
Strong competitive credibility through Portugal's Superprestígio series
weaknesses: Official sources conflict on distances (140.3/105.4/71.1 km vs. 135/100/69 km elsewhere in the organizer's own materials)
Total Granfondo elevation gain is not published anywhere found — treat any figure as an estimate
The route required a reroute after winter landslides damaged the traditional northern roads
All three distances start together with only about 2 km controlled, creating a potentially crowded opening
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A genuinely scenic, well-organized Portuguese fondo with real competitive pedigree — just don't expect the organizer's own numbers to agree with each other, and confirm the current year's route given recent weather-forced changes.

final_verdict.one_liner: Portuguese granfondo blending Douro climbs with wine-country scenery — genuinely well-attended, but the organizer's own course figures don't agree with each other.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes for the scenery, international field and Superprestígio pedigree. Confirm the current year's exact route and distance directly — recent landslide damage forced a reroute, and published figures conflict.
final_verdict.alternatives: For similarly scenic wine-region climbing in Iberia, compare against other Portuguese or Spanish Superprestígio-series gran fondos.

youtube_data.search_query: Love Tiles Douro Granfondo gran fondo cycling Peso da Régua

videos.title: Love Tiles Douro Granfondo 2017
videos.description:
Love Tiles Douro Granfondo 2017  - Tema: Amizade! 

Este ano o tema era amizade! Levei companhia para a viagem até Peso da Régua e regresso, e depois fiz o belo percurso 156km de bicicleta que demorou sensivelmente 7h07min! 

Um percurso duro mas agradável a nível de paisagens:) 

Music: Shed a Light - Robin Schulz & David Guetta & Cheat Codes

videos.title: Love Tiles Douro Granfondo 2016
videos.description:
60km - 1440D+ - 02:04:23 - 47º Geral Minifondo

1 de Maio de 2016 , Peso da Régua.
A partir de uma GoPro instalada na bicicleta, aqui fica um pequeno registo da minha estreia neste género de provas de ciclismo amador. Este ano no total estavam 3000 inscritos para os três percursos possíveis o MiniFondo ( 60km ) MedioFondo ( 112km ) e o Granfondo ( 162 km ). Grande festa, parabéns à organização!!!
